URL: https://www.worcesternews.co.uk/news/19130211.drug-dealers-pair-caught-worcester-900-worth-cannabis-avoid-jail/
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Drug dealers caught in Worcester with 900 worth of ...

CONTENT: TWO drug dealers have been sentenced by a judge after being caught by police with ? 900 worth of cannabis on them . Hayley Baker admitted possession of a controlled drug of class B , cannabis , with intent to supply when she appeared at Worcester Crown Court on Friday , while Benjamin Spares had pleaded guilty to the offence at an earlier hearing . Alison Scott-Jones , prosecuting , said a black Audi was stopped on Rainbow Hill , Worcester , at around noon on April 10 , 2019 . " The driver was Hayley Baker and the passenger was Benjamin Spares , " the prosecutor said . " When the officers approached they noticed the strong smell of cannabis . " PC Fowler found a number of items that included a cannister that contained cannabis , four bags of cannabis , five plastic bags of cannabis , four containers with amounts of cannabis , six small pots that had cannabis sweets , 18 bags of sweets that were infused with cannabis , 10 bags of cannabis infused chocolate , and a bottle of cannabis syrup . " The total value @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ " The prosecutor said drug related paraphernalia was found in the car , including scales , a cannabis grinder and dealer bags . Miss Scott-Jones explained a mobile phone was also found , which was later examined and showed a significant amount of messages with requests for drugs and the offering of cannabis for sale . The prosecutor said the previous convictions of Baker , formerly of Lower Moor , Pershore and now living in Plymouth , were for drug driving . The Worcester News reported on that case when Baker was given a curfew and a driving ban after being caught twice in just over a week drug driving in Worcester , in April 2019 . Miss Scott-Jones said Spares , 21 , had six convictions for eight offences , including for possession of a class A drug . The paper reported on Spares , of Bromyard Road , Worcester , a year ago when he received a suspended prison sentence for urinating and spraying sauce over the belongings of a neighbour during a burglary . Jason Aris , defending Spares , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ offence pre-dated the conviction for burglary , when a suspended prison sentence was given to him . Judge James Burbridge replied : " This puts me in a difficult position as I thought in the case immediate custody was appropriate , but last year he was given a suspended sentence which he has not breached . " Mr Aris said Spares had no offences of a similar nature on his record and he had referred himself to Cranstoun drug centre to address his issues with taking cannabis . " There are signs he is finally starting to turn a corner , " Mr Aris added . Martin Butterworth , defending Baker , said she was now working in the care industry and had moved out of the area to make a new life for herself . The judge asked " Has she given up cannabis ? " to which Mr Butterworth replied : " I believe she has . " Sentencing the pair , the judge spoke of his concern of the cannabis being in different forms including sweets that could of been of @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ considered to have a leading role in the case , but he recognised Spears had abided by the terms of the suspended sentence . " You are keeping out of trouble , you are trying to settle down , " the judge said . " I hope you have given up cannabis . " Spears was given an eight month prison sentence , suspended for 12 months . He received a two month curfew , active between the hours of 8pm and 7am nightly , was ordered to carry out 200 hours unpaid work , and pay costs of ? 300 . Baker was handed a 12 month community order that included 30 rehabilitation activity requirement days , and was told to pay costs of ? 300 . " Miss Baker it seems you have a real habit for taking cannabis - I have to trust you will change your lifestyle , " the judge added . COUNTRY: ie
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: EntertainmentTG4 coverage last year brought Gerry to fleadh1 hour ago

CONTENT: TG4 coverage last year brought Gerry to fleadh Gerry Farrell , who lives in Drumcondra , was at the fleadh on Thursday . He had n't been to a fleadh in around 50 years , but he told the Westmeath Examiner that after watching last year 's coverage on TG4 , he was compelled to make the journey to Mullingar . " I remember being impressed by a c ? il ? band from Glasgow , " said Gerry . " I 'm interested in the music ... The different people , the variety of people , I suppose from the Irish diaspora . It 's a way of coming together , is n't it ? " Gerry travelled by rail and returned to Dublin again that evening . The trip in itself was enjoyable , as lots of fellow train passengers were headed to the fleadh . " Even coming down here on the train , there was two ladies beside me from Dundalk , and an old guy got on in Enfield and he knew musicians -- I was only sorry I had n't got the phone on record -- because @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ walking encyclopedia ... a character . " And the two old ladies , they were impressed with him because one of them , I think , was a serious musician . ' Do you know such and such ? ' ' Oh , I know him , and blah , blah , blah . ' He knew everybody , " said Gerry . " I have n't attended a fleadh since , it would have been the ' 70s , late ' 60s ... and they were provincial fleadhanna , small . It 's incredible , I mean a week of music ! I 'm looking forward to seeing some great music . "

URL: https://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/2017/dec/10/surely-pornography-has-no-place-at-work
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Surely porn has no place at work? Not everyone thinks so

CONTENT: What with the unsocial hours and media persecution , on top of prime minister 's questions , in-party factionalism and the prevailing incompatibility between political advancement and any discernible ability , there is already justifiable concern about the recruitment of talented MPs . To these standard deterrents we must now add the very real danger that promising young multitaskers , hoping to fit a parliamentary career around a demanding pornography habit , will no longer think it worthwhile . How , it is being asked , will we ever again attract leaders of Churchill 's calibre if they are not allowed to lighten the burden with regular sexual stimulation ? Thanks to Harvey Weinstein , after all , they are already barred from workplace knee-touching . How can Westminster possibly tempt the finest minds without guaranteed office access to Pornhub ? Not for anything approaching the " extreme " , illegal sort , of course : no responsible public servant would wish to download anything other than bona fide legal pornography , at least during working hours . And if that 's still a problem , for individuals soon ( given current trends ) to be @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Westminster to instal some sort of obscenity measuring software , to protect MPs from accidentally exciting themselves with questionable material . I called Hugh Hefner a pimp , he threatened to sue . But that 's what he was Suzanne Moore Read more Setting aside , pending a parliamentary report , questions of whether Damian Green , the first secretary of state , did or did not , as has been alleged , lie about downloading pornography or behave inappropriately towards women , the reaction to these claims has itself been instructive , possibly even revelatory , to anyone who imagined a consensus existed on the viewing of pornography at work . Even in a workplace such as Westminster , where a professional addresses his secretary as " sugar tits " . In fact , however dimly Cressida Dick , the Metropolitan police commissioner , might view their conduct , were it not for the two retired police officers who have alleged finding pornography on Green 's computer in the course of an unrelated 2008 inquiry , some of us might still be unaware how widespread is @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ a problem with pornography at work . To that extent , the retired officers surely performed a public service . It 's thanks to their disclosures , and the defence of at-work pornography these have prompted , that officials running the Westminster estate may want to abandon the blocking of ( 110,000 per year ) attempts to view pornographic sites , since that activity is not , as Iain Duncan Smith reminds us , " illegal " . Had it not been for the officers ' allegations , much of the public might never have learned , courtesy of an academic guest on Newsnight , that , at some point yet to be disclosed , workplace porn viewing was found to be indistinguishable from online shopping . You would n't report someone for searching Ocado or Argos . Anyone disturbed by the sight on a co-worker 's screen of a porn star 's penis should just think of it as , say , a pot of low-fat yoghurt or a toaster . Anyone disturbed by the sight of a porn star 's penis should just think of it @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ That 's not just a quicker but also , according to numerous authoritative figures , a more proportionate reaction than would be reporting sexual harassment under the 2010 Equality Act : " Unwanted conduct of a sexual nature which has the purpose or effect of violating someone 's dignity , or creating an intimidating , hostile , degrading , humiliating or offensive environment for them . " True , recent reporting of Hugh Hefner 's death , where it preposterously recited the old pimp 's claim to have advanced women 's interests , had already confirmed that a super-tolerant approach to ubiquitous pornography coexists , if it does not yet eclipse , concerns about objectification or criminality . It was only to be expected , then , that some liberated voices would demand that critics of workplace porning just get over ourselves . Crispin Blunt , noted campaigner for legalised prostitution , was among the first to rally round . MPs such as Green and himself " simply do not have time " , he said , for what is in any case , " legal activity " @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ let Green deputise for May , while under investigation , at PMQs . And even taking into account his earlier " It 's DD for me " promotion , and sexually objectifying abuse of Diane Abbott , David Davis 's participation in this normalising of porn use indicated a hitherto unguessed-at passion for sexual liberation in the workplace . Presumably he takes issue with concern within his party , voiced not long ago by Baroness Shields , the prime minister 's special representative on internet crime and harms , that " pornography and an increasingly sexualised culture more generally are noted as a ' conducive context ' , perpetrating certain stereotypes , particularly about men dominating women " . Davis , the Evening Standard reported , is so far unworried by the Equality Act , as applied to possibly offensive environments , that he had " put his cloak around " Green , even threatened resignation " if Damian was pushed out because of anything related to what happened 10 years ago " . If that extreme chivalry does not quite align with assertions from his own @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ must surely dishearten the teachers , unions and HR departments that are attempting to increase understanding about inappropriate sexual behaviour . It 's only a few months , after all , since London Transport 's head of transport policing , Siwan Hayward , felt the need to make it clear , for women who have been upset by proximity to porn-viewing passengers , that : " We do not tolerate any unwanted sexual behaviour on our transport network . " On the other side , men in future evicted from trains -- or from public service -- because of unwanted sexual behaviour , may benefit from their hobby 's new endorsement , by senior politicians and opinion-formers , as completely acceptable . Could there even be a case for retrospective pardons , courtesy of this rethink , for some of the dismissed teachers , council workers ( including in Green 's constituency ) , police officers and medics found guilty of misconduct ? Should n't there be justice , if punishing an MP for downloading porn would be , in principle , " unfair " , for the three @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ ruled , by the lord chancellor and lord chief justice , " inexcusable " and " wholly unacceptable conduct for a judicial office holder " ? Alternatively , of course , our legislators could , instead of formulating exceptionalist excuses , choose to submit to the same codes of conduct as civilians . Regardless of Green 's activities , the insistence on the part of some senior MPs that viewing pornography at work could not constitute , if it 's them doing it , a form of sexual harassment , is indicative , as much as anything , of their continuing drift away from civilised norms . They do not , presumably , propose extending similar opportunities to likeminded workers in other trades , with private porn breaks for frustrated ambulance drivers , adult surfing time for Amazon 's stressed-out warehouse operatives . Already licensed to be , in some prominent cases , bullying , abusive , idle , thick , incompetent and duplicitous , to a degree inconceivable in any other form of legal employment , MPs are further demanding a privilege previously reserved for workers in @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Phillips has defined as the right to " wank at work " .

COUNTRY: gb

TITLE: Scottish Budget: Government promises Dundee custody unit ...

CONTENT: In the budget statement , the Scottish Government says : " While Scotland 's prisons are recognised as being well run , we acknowledge that they face a number of significant challenges -- in particular the consequences of a rapid increase in the prison population from 2018-19 . " While compared with a decade ago , far fewer people now receive custodial sentences each year , those that do on average receive longer sentences and are increasingly complex to manage . " In 2020-21 we will progress transformation of the female custodial estate to address the specific needs of female offenders , including the construction of a smaller national facility at HMP Cornton Vale and the development of two innovative community-based custody units in Glasgow and Dundee before the end of 2021 . URL: https://uk.reuters.com/markets/deals/twitter-ceo-says-two-leaders-will-depart-company-hiring-paused-2022-05-12/
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Twitter CEO says two leaders to depart, hiring paused amid Musk takeover

CONTENT: U.S. stocks ended a whipsaw session slightly lower on Thursday , as investors juggled signs of peaking inflation with fears that it could remain elevated , prompting ever more aggressive tightening from the Federal Reserve . Finland said on Thursday it would apply to join NATO " without delay " , with Sweden expected to follow , as Russia 's invasion of Ukraine looked set to bring about the very expansion of the Western military alliance that Vladimir Putin aimed to prevent . The meltdown in TerraUSD , one of the world 's largest stablecoins , sent shockwaves through cryptocurrency markets on Thursday , pushing another major stablecoin Tether below its dollar peg and sending bitcoin to 16-month lows . Two senior Twitter leaders who oversee the consumer and revenue divisions will depart the social media company , chief executive Parag Agrawal told employees in a memo on Thursday , in one of the biggest shake-ups at the firm since billionaire Elon Musk announced he would buy Twitter for $44 billion . URL: https://www.theguardian.com/lifeandstyle/2016/aug/14/nigel-slater-blackcurrant-recipes
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Nigel Slater's blackcurrant recipes

CONTENT: This column has ... been devoted to the pleasures of everyday cooking : a celebration of the simple , seasonal and satisfying , with barely so much as a nod to what you could call hobby cooking . So , nothing really for those who like to produce their own salami . But earlier this week I got a fancy to make baked Alaska , the simultaneously hot and cold dessert with layers of golden sponge and ice cream under a cloud of hot meringue that no one could exactly call " everyday " . It is a once-in-a-blue-moon way to end a meal for the both the eater and the cook , which is probably why I had so much fun making it . One can be too down to earth sometimes . If I 'm honest , I find the classic baked Alaska on the sweet side . With that in mind I have added a sharp sauce of blackcurrants to pour into the meringue as you shatter its sparkly crust with your spoon . Yes , such recipes use every damn bowl and pan in the kitchen and I had to rethink my usual rather laid-back speed of @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ your Alaska is not to end in sweet , creamy , tears . ) But the result is gorgeous and left us sinking our spoons through meringue into cold ice cream and soft sponge cake like seven-year-olds at a birthday party . Do n't even think of using shop-bought cake . The sort of cook who has a blow torch will find the final bit of the recipe a doddle . Those who have n't , like me , may have to keep their fingers crossed . Ice cream in the oven , what could possibly go wrong ? Blackcurrant compote blackcurrants 350gcaster sugar 80gwater 3 tbsp Remove the blackcurrants from their stalks , dropping them into a small saucepan . Add the sugar and water , then bring to the boil . Lower the heat , and let the currants simmer gently for 4 or 5 minutes , then remove from the heat as soon as the sugar has dissolved and the berries are starting to burst . Pour into a jug or bowl and set aside . A sense of urgency is required @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ baking tray or swiss roll tin approx 30 x 22cm , lined with baking parchment . Set the oven at 170C/gas mark 3-4 . Put the butter into the bowl of a food mixer , add the sugar and beat till light , creamy and soft . Occasionally scrape down the sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula to help the ingredients to combine smoothly . Break the eggs into a bowl , beat lightly with a fork to mix the yolks and whites then add to the butter and sugar a little at a time , the beater still turning , till the eggs are fully incorporated . Mix the flour and baking powder then fold into the batter . Transfer the mixture to the lined baking tray , smooth the surface then scatter the blackcurrants on top . Bake in the preheated oven for 35 minutes ( check after 30 ) . Remove from the oven , leave to cool then turn out on to a cooling rack and peel off the paper . When the sponge is cool enough , cut out @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ ) . Transfer the discs to a parchment-lined baking sheet , leaving a little room around each . Make 6 large balls of ice cream with a scoop , place them on a metal or plastic tray and return them to the freezer until hard . To make the meringue , put the sugar on a baking sheet and warm in a low oven for 5-7 minutes . Whisk the egg whites in a clean food mixer till thick and fluffy . With the whisk still turning , add the warmed sugar in 2 or 3 batches , then continue whisking for 4 or 5 minutes till firm and glossy . Fold in the cornflour . Set the oven at 220C/gas mark 7 . Press the centre of each sponge disc with the back of a spoon to create a hollow . Working quickly , place a ball of ice cream in the centre of each piece , then cover each with a thick layer of meringue , enclosing the entire cake , right down to the baking sheet . There should be no cake or ice @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ for 3-5 minutes till the meringue starts to colour . Alternatively , if you have a kitchen blowtorch , lightly brown each one all over . Lift on to cool plates and serve with the compote ( above ) . Put the water and sugar into a medium-sized saucepan and bring to the boil . Cut the peaches in half , remove the stones , then lower the fruit into the syrup . Reduce the heat and leave to simmer for 7-10 minutes or until the peaches are fully tender . Remove the peaches from the pan with a draining spoon and place them snugly , hollow side up , in a serving dish . Place a tablespoon of mascarpone in each peach half . Crumble the amaretti on top . Warm a little compote in a pan , then spoon over the peaches and serve .

URL: http://metro.co.uk/2017/11/15/bella-hadid-shows-off-sister-gigi-after-she-wins-glamour-woman-of-the-year-award-7080360/
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Bella Hadid shows off sister Gigi after she wins Glamour Woman Of ...

CONTENT: The girls and their mother attended the Glamour Woman Of The Year awards in NYC , where Gigi was awarded with the Supernova award , and the pride her family felt at her win oozed out of every pore . Bella could n't even resist taking a moment to show off her big sister , making a big deal of the 22-year-old for the red carpet cameras , and her mum Yolanda was by her side too . That gold dress is our new obsession . It was a family affair ( Picture : Backgrid ) The siblings even wore sisterly outfits , with Bella dressed in silver beside Gigi 's gold -- letting the older Hadid take the more precious colour on her big night . Gigi Hadid was flanked by her family on her big night ( Picture : Mega Agency ) Honouring her sister on Instagram , Bella wrote : ' Always got your back . guys , how lucky am I that I get to have my own personal princess as a sister ! ! ! ? ? ! Congratulations my beautiful G to be recognized as Glamour @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ and proud to call you my best friend , sister and icon . ' The @glamourmag Awards were , yet again , so inspiring , emotional and powerful . Thank you to all of the genius , incredible , strong , mega inspiring , beautiful and unique women who spoke and congratulations to all of the women who were acknowledged for their bravery , talent and strength . Such a beautiful event . I love you GStar ! ' Speaking prior to getting the award , Gigi said on the Today show : ' I mean , I 've heard so many good things about the event and every year people show up just to hear what the women have to say and it 's always such a powerful and emotional night . ' So I 'm so excited and it 's such an honor . It 's kind of crazy I 'm still kind of surprised . ' Serena Williams presented her with the award on the night -- admitting she would only ever leave her newborn for Gigi . Serena Williams said on @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ the few people on this planet I would leave my baby for -- for one night -- but for you it shows how much you mean to me . And I want you to imagine the affect you have on the world . ' Got a story ? URL: http://www.telegraph.co.uk/foodanddrink/restaurants/7835321/Restaurant-critic-Matthew-Norman-makes-his-debut.html
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Restaurant critic Matthew Norman makes his debut

CONTENT: Matthew Norman reveals the highs and lows of eating out and some of Britain 's best ( and worst ) tables . There are certain trades that cause their occupiers to recoil when they hear the icebreaker " So what do you do ? " at a party . For some it 's the fear of being thumped ( BP 's head of environmental integrity at a Cajun barbecue ) , for others of being bullied for free professional advice ( " A proctologist ! I do n't suppose you 've brought your latex gloves ? Only I 've noticed this worrying ... " ) . Others want to avoid the flash of malevolent envy in the questioner 's eyes . For us poor restaurant critics , it 's the full bag . Tell someone you get paid to eat out and one of three reactions will follow . I list them , in the style of Eric Morley , in reverse order of frequency . " Ah , so you 're the one whose rave review sent us to that sushi place in the West Midlands . " " Yup , that was me . How was it ? " @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ so well to the E-coli drugs that she 'll be off life support in time for Christmas . Would you care to step outside ? " Another typical response ? " Oh really , in that case can I pick your brains ? My cousin Concepci ? n is stopping by on her way home to Lima from Tashkent , where she 's been working as a yak herd . Can you recommend somewhere within 70 yards of Stansted airport that does great Peruvian-Uzbek fusion cooking at ? 4.95 a head , including coffee , wine , service and ethnic dancing ? " And the most common of all ? " You lucky ------- . I could do that . Anyone could do that . " And anyone , of course , could . Tempting as it is to protest that this is a highly specialised critical form , requiring a palate of exquisite sensitivity and a knowledge of culinary technique to make Heston Blumenthal resemble the trainee microwave operator at Burger King , the truth is otherwise . All that 's required is a mouth , @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ about much more than its food and a reliable instinct ( touch wood ) as to whether the reader would think a visit worth the time and expense . But perhaps I self-deprecate too far . Useful qualities are the ability to ignore a reputation , however stellar ; sharp antennae for pretension ; and an editor robust enough to treat outraged restaurateurs with the insouciant disregard they deserve . It seems to me that the British will put up with anything on a plate to avoid causing a scene . The template is the old biddies in Fawlty Towers moaning about their gristle-laden stew . When Basil asks if they 're enjoying their dinner , they twitter : " Oh yes , Mr Fawlty , very nice thank you . " Almost four decades later , the greater freedom of emotional expression identified since the death of Diana , Princess of Wales , has yet to permeate the world of eating out . In this one area of national life , the upper lip remains stiff despite the alleged foodie revolution . The British still lack @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ bottle of wine . Mind you , given the standards of service that persist , sullen acceptance has its charms . A few days ago I asked the manager of the Royal China in Bayswater , supposedly among Britain 's finest Chinese restaurants , to replace a noodle dish drowned in a globulous and repulsive gravy . He ended the ensuing shouting match with a Fawltian : " Rude ? I can be much ruder than this . " Perhaps if I 'd been working he would have been more affable , because if one thing has the power to transform service in an instant , it 's the notebook . Available from your newsagent for as little as 59p , this alchemic device is worth its weight in 24-carat gold , and you should carry one at all times . One flash of lined paper and you 'll have the staff at your beck and call . Nothing is foolproof , of course . I had the notebook out one evening when we went to the cavernous Indian restaurant Khan 's on Westbourne Grove in west @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ incivility of its waiters as Soho 's legendary Wong Kei . Our starters had just arrived when we watched a spat develop between a customer and a waiter . The latter had gone that extra mile for his silver service certificate by pouring -- accidentally , I assume -- a pint of lager over the former . The customer threw a punch , the waiter defended himself , all the customer 's friends waded in , as did the waiter 's colleagues , trying to restore order . What followed reminded me of a saloon bar brawl in which chairs are smashed and bodies crash through tables . We pinpointed the arrival of a dozen coppers as a good time to pop off to the rival curry house next door . Half an hour later I nipped in to pay for what little we 'd eaten and speak to the manager . " The manager is off . He 's playing golf . " Of course , I said , as a couple of lager-drenched yobs were led away in handcuffs . With everything running so smoothly who @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ be amazed to learn , be prickly . At a dinner for an ITV restaurant awards show , a fellow judge introduced me to Tom Aikens , as taciturn a man as ever sauced a turbot . In a bid to cheer him up , I facetiously asked if he was one of those kitchen tyrants who hit the sous-chef with a frying pan or branded them . The wind whistled through the room and peals of distant church bells were audible as my colleague gently steered me away . " You do know why he was fired from Pied ? Terre , do n't you ? " she said . " No . Tell me it was n't the frying pan , " I said . Her reply ? " He was accused of branding someone with a palette knife . " Restaurantkitchens are intensely hot and stressful places , so allowances must be made for the odd flash of temper ( at least by those of us not sporting livid red scars on the upper arm ) or a misplaced show of imperiousness . At a @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ sat in a giant amphora facing a spectacularly tumescent pastiche of Michelangelo 's David , a waiter refused to remove our barely touched main courses on the grounds that " chef likes to see the plates cleared " . I 'd like a washboard stomach and a full head of hair , I pointed out , but as the philosopher Jagger posits , you ca n't always get what you want . A quirk of the acoustics then permitted us to hear that chef bitching about us for the next 10 minutes . Harder to excuse from chefs than ill humour is ludicrous flourishes designed to gratify Michelin inspectors rather than human beings . Too often that foodie bible proves the Book of the Apocrypha , awarding and withholding stars according to criteria that make no sense in the real world . The commercial ( and egomaniacal ) pressure on chefs to snaffle stars entices them into all those daft touches -- the carsick foams , the baby-food pur ? es , the green slime ringing the plate to suggest the discharge of a snail mortally wounded in the @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ joint transported from Ludlow to Mayfair , we met a dish that encapsulates the nonsense as well as any : Cornish monkfish tail , cassoulet of Paimpol beans , shallot pur ? e and -- hold on to your hats now , here it comes -- emulsion of Douglas Firs . It 's wonderful that the essence of a Herefordshire pine tree can now be sprayed from an aerosol onto a plate , no one would argue with that . But the fact that something has become technically possible does n't mean you have to do it . The good news is that the rampant foolishness is in decline , which may be one entry to be made on the profit side of the austerity age ledger . What seemed comically absurd in the boom times looks more like callous taunting now and there has been a discernible swing towards the pleasingly earthy in the last couple of years . Aubergine caviar is a distant memory now . The dish du jour of late has been chicken and leek pie . For all that , and despite clear @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ and rural areas , and thanks in part to the rise of the gastropub -- restaurant meals remain overpriced . The French still have a lot to teach us about bargain set menus , not to mention adding a decent profit to the cost of a bottle of wine without tripling or quadrupling its wholesale price . Far too often , promising cooks undermine their gift in futile homage to Heston Blumenthal or Ferran Adri ? that only spotlights the vast chasm between ambition and genius . While too frequently , serious talents diminish themselves in the quest for fame and wealth , the slow motion decline of Gordon Ramsay might be a morality tale about the perils of greed and egomania ( here 's one for Pseud 's Corner ) from the pen of Tolstoy . Gary Rhodes , once such a likeable and refreshing enthusiast for gutsy British cooking , is damaging his reputation through the avaricious franchising of his name to soulless corporate enterprises . In part , I blame the word " chef " itself . If we 'd resisted the lure of Gallic @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ 'd have stuck to what they do best . The role model should n't be Alain Ducasse , Joel Robuchon or any of the Michelin deities , but Mrs Kate Bridges of Eaton Square . In Upstairs , Downstairs , she understood that a cook should be neither seen nor heard , other than to rebuke Ruby for breaking another china cup . Only once did Mrs B do what the grand chefs of today do all the time , and bothered her diners . And that was because George V summoned her from below to congratulate her on her baron of lamb and reward her with a golden sovereign . If the pram in the hallway is the enemy of art , the giant ego in the kitchen is the good restaurant 's fiercest foe . It seduces the cook into wanting to show off and erodes the notion that simplicity is king . The most reliable formula for success -- take first-rate ingredients , cook them accurately and without fuss so they taste vibrantly of themselves , and do n't leave the punters feeling ripped off @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ there is , as I said , so much more to it than the cooking . There are places that turn out glorious food to which you 'd never return , because of snarky or sycophantic service , bad acoustics , interrogation-cell lighting or hideous decor . Others we go back to time and again , even though the food is far from special , because they make us feel relaxed and jolly . Deciding what makes a good , bad or mediocre restaurant is a matter of instinct and there are no iron rules . Well , perhaps just the one . If a politician recommends somewhere , avoid it as though it were a character in a radiation suit bearing a chunk of toxic waste . Never have the spirits sunk as far or fast as they did when I entered Gordon Brown 's favourite Cantonese in Fife , after a 500-mile drive , to hear the life president of the Rab C Nesbitt Fan Club ordering " a quadruple vodka and Irn Bru , hen , and the chicken curry with chips . " @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ understand , is this the hardest game in the world . FIVE MENU IRRITANTS " Pan-fried " fillets of lemon sole -- what , like they might be fried in a dustbin lid ? Milk-fed lamb -- it 's a baby animal , what else would it be fed ? Enriched uranium ? Assiette of Gressingham duck -- if it 's a plate , call it a plate . " Handmade " pasta in smart Italians -- at ? 22 for the dish , would we expect Buitoni ? Name-checking **25;663;TOOLONG shop -- I could n't be less interested FIVE PERSONAL FAVOURITES Amaya , Knightsbridge Poshed-up Indian street food theatrically cooked on griddles and in tandoors before your very eyes . By no means cheap , but you 'll never have tasted the like in all your life . Patio , Goldhawk Road , London W12 Vast servings of good Polish stodge , with coffee and vodka thrown in , served with endless warmth . The perfect neighbourhood joint . Morston Hall , Holt , Norfolk Galton Blackiston not only @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ elegant , consistently beautiful food ( and teaches ) in a superb modern country house hotel . Sir Charles Napier , Chinnor , Oxon Glorious cooking in a glorious old inn at gloriously low prices ( weekday set menu for lunch and dinner is ? 14.50 ) . Take the satnav . FIVE OF THE WORST MEALS IN MEMORY Colony Bar and Grill , London W1 Hideous , inedible Anglo-Raj pseudo-tapas served by clueless hoverers in a chillingly clinical room . " But chef says it 's fine , " said the manager on the return of our main courses untouched . Yes , but the chef does n't have to eat it . The Moody Goose at the Old Priory Hotel , Midsomer Norton near Bath The doe-eyed sweetness of the teenage waiter compelled us to remove almost everything and flush it away to avoid causing distress . Twee ? They call the loo " the Abbot 's " , and adorn the loo paper with a pink bow . Shepherd 's of Westminster Noxiously self-aggrandising politician 's trough that may have improved dramatically @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ since the only way I 'd be let in now is if I was disguised in a burka . Mr Chu 's China Palace , Hull John Prescott 's most loved restaurant , which ought to tell you more than enough . Seventeenth-rate Chinese cooking served in a glacial , cavernous space on an industrial estate . The Palm , London SW1 Monstrously self-reverential British pioneer of well known American steakhouse chain . Wickedly overpriced and mediocre meat served by staff who talked incessantly . One waiter leant over to remove a piece of fluff from my friend 's shoulder . " Do n't worry , " he said . " It is n't dandruff . "

TITLE: Oncimmune Holdings PLC welcomes NHS review  of  cancer ...

CONTENT: Oncimmune welcomes NHS review of cancer screening programmes The review is aiming to increase earlier cancer diagnosis among patients , something the company is trying to achieve with its EarlyCDT lung cancer test EarlyCDT was recently used in a trial of 12,000 patients at high risk of developing lung cancer Oncimmune Holdings PLC ( LON:ONC ) has welcomed recommendations from an independent review into NHS cancer screening programmes , which has suggested the entire system needs overhauling to aid earlier diagnosis and treatment . Adam Hill , chief executive of the AIM-listed firm , said the review had made " sensible , practical recommendations " towards making early cancer diagnosis more accessible to patients . Oncimmune is developing EarlyCDT , a test that can detect lung cancer four years earlier than standard clinical diagnosis using blood samples , and is currently trying roll it out across the UK following a trial among 12,000 high-risk patients in Scotland which showed that more people taking the test were diagnosed with early stages of lung cancer in the subsequent two years than those who received only standard clinical care . " Our test for lung @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ available right now . It has the potential to save lives as part of a targeted screening program as well as significantly reducing costs for the NHS . We look forward to continuing to work with screening experts throughout the UK to roll EarlyCDT Lung out more widely " , Hill said . TITLE: Three people arrested after convoy of stolen vehicles intercepted and seized

CONTENT: Police have arrested three people after intercepting a convoy of what they believe to be stolen vehicles with fake number plates . North Yorkshire Police arrested three people from the Cleveland area at around 5.30pm on Thursday ( 9 March ) , two men and a woman , all in their 30s , after receiving a tip-off . Three vehicles were apparently travelling in convoy on the A19 and A168 southbound near Thirsk . A Nissan Navara , a Range Rover Sport and a Mercedes van that was carrying two Land Rovers were stopped by officers in Sharow , near Ripon . If you want to read more great stories , why not subscribe to your Northern Echo for as little as ? 1.25 a week . Click here On closer inspection of the vehicles , several were suspected to have fake vehicle identification numbers ( VINs ) . These are numbers that are stamped into the body of the car which allow authorities and insurers to track vehicle histories more definitively than with number plates alone . The woman was further arrested on suspicion of driving whilst disqualified . All vehicles @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ forensic examination and further enquiries . TITLE: M62 delays: Heavy traffic on motorway as drivers warned

CONTENT: Get the latest MEN breaking news on WhatsApp Drivers have been urged to allow for extra time if using the M62 motorway this morning as repairs are carried out . One lane is closed off on the eastbound carriageway near junction 25 for Brighouse . This is due to a carriageway defect , National Highways said . Emergency repair works are being carried out , with the lane closure expected to remain closed throughout the morning . Motorway cameras show heavy traffic up to the incident , with congestion currently back to junction 23 . In a post on X at 5.20am , National Highways said : " Lane 3 ( of 3 ) will be closed throughout the morning on the #M62 eastbound within J25 ( #Brighouse ) due to a carriageway defect . Please allow extra time if travelling through the area . At this time , all lanes are closed within the junction but lanes 1 & 2 will be opening shortly . " A post at 7am added : " Lane 3 ( of 3 ) is closed on the #M62 eastbound within J25 ( #Brighouse ) due to @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ through the area . " Yesterday afternoon , drivers faced two hours delays on the M62 after a car towing a trailer overturned . The crash happened on the eastbound stretch of the motorway , heading from Greater Manchester towards Yorkshire , between junction 22 for Rishworth Moor and junction 23 for Huddersfield , shortly after midday . Thankfully no one was left injured . Huge queues formed as lanes were closed off , with drivers facing delays of up to two hours into the afternoon .

TITLE: Former ambulance station in Whitchurch can be demolished for new homesWhitchurch | 8 hours ago

CONTENT: Former ambulance station in Whitchurch can be demolished for new homes A former ambulance station in north Shropshire can be demolished to make way for five new homes despite objections , council planners have decided . Subscribe to our daily newsletter ! Whitchurch ambulance station is set to be demolished for housing The ambulance station at Queensway , in Whitchurch , was vacated in 2013 following a restructuring of the service and has been used for storage purposes since then . Shropshire Council planners said that even though Whitchurch has already exceeded targets to find room for 1,200 homes during the local planning period , five more would not cause harm to the " operation of local services and infrastructure " . They say Whitchurch has already found sites for 1,390 homes , either with planning permission or allocated for development . Previous plans for residential development on the site had been rejected for technical reasons but this time the proposal was to establish the principle . Issues of access , appearance , landscaping , layout and scale are all reserved for later approval . One objector to the plan @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ for his van . He said there is nowhere else " in the town to park a vehicle securely without worrying nothing is going to be pinched off it " . He also claimed there were bats in the area . Other objectors were concerned about significant disturbance from dust and noise and possible damage , as well as inconvenience and danger to pedestrians . " The old ambulance station has been at this location since 1977 . It has become a landmark in the area and would be such a shame to see it go , " they said . Planning officers took the decision without reference to the planning committee because the proposed development was " considered to accord with the requirements of the council 's relevant policies and no comments contrary to officer opinion have been received from the Parish Council " . A condition has been imposed so that the site is assessed for potential contamination before development commences .

URL: https://eveningtimes.co.uk/news/23347937.mike-dailly-stop-failing-patients-comes-human-rights/
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: Mike Dailly: Stop failing patients when it comes to human rights

CONTENT: This had never been discussed with him or any family member ; and as a patient , he had full legal capacity to give consent to medical treatment . DNR means if a person suffers from a heart attack and stops breathing while in hospital they will not be resuscitated . To be fair , the nurse removed this from his case record after discussing it with him . He 's trying to find out how this happened . Perhaps an oversight or administrative error . But it 's a pretty gaping failure by any standard . If you 're in hospital you 're there for medical treatment ; if you 're seriously ill or undergoing a lifesaving procedure and have legal capacity why would n't a doctor or nurse obtain your views and consent on the issue of resuscitation ? Without doubt this is a sensitive subject , but it 's one that 's vitally important to all of us as human beings as a matter of dignity and human rights . DNR decisions became very controversial during the Covid-19 pandemic . In 2021 , the English Care Quality Commission ( CQC @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ a blanket basis in the first wave of the pandemic . The CQC has expressed concern over the relationship between poor practice and the unprecedented pressure on the NHS . The found poor record keeping , and a lack of oversight and scrutiny of decisions being made -- importantly , these problems pre-dated the pandemic . As regards the pandemic , the use of DNR decisions during Covid-19 are within the terms of reference of the UK Covid-19 inquiry and indeed the Scottish Covid-19 public inquiry . It is deeply troubling to think that poor practice is still occurring over DNR decisions . Not least , because the law in this area has been clear for almost a decade . The law was clarified by the England and Wales Court of Appeal in R ( Tracey ) v Cambridge University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust and others 2014 EWCA Civ 822 . Janet Tracey had been diagnosed with terminal lung cancer in February 2011 . Later that month she was in a car crash . She was taken to hospital with a neck fracture then transferred @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ 7 March 2011 . Her husband David brought a judicial review in the English High Court . He sought to clarify whether there was a legal duty to inform patients with capacity whether a DNR had been placed on their record and whether they had any right to be consulted about it . His late wife had two DNR orders placed on her medical notes without her consent and without anyone discussing this with her . The English Court of Appeal found that doctors had acted unlawfully when they had placed a DNACPR on Mrs Tracey 's medical record . Lord Dyson , the Master of the Rolls , said : A " DNACPR decision is one which will potentially deprive the patient of life-saving treatment , there should be a presumption in favour of patient involvement . There need to be convincing reasons not to involve the patient . " The court made it clear that doctors should be wary of being " too ready to exclude patients from the process on the grounds that their involvement is likely to distress them " . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ consult ... involves a discussion , where practicable , about the patient 's wishes and feelings that is better undertaken at the earliest stages of the clinical relationship so that decisions can be reviewed as circumstances change . That involves an acknowledgement that the duty to consult is integral to the respect for the dignity of the patient . " The Tracey case was followed by Winspear v City Hospitals Sunderland NHS Foundation Trust 2015 EWHC 3250 . A young man with cerebral palsy had been admitted to a hospital with a chest infection and a DNR notice was placed on his record without any consultation with his mother and carer . The young man did not have legal capacity to consent to a DNR but the court found that the hospital ought to have consulted with his carer . The failure to do so was a breach of the patient 's article 8 right for respect for his private life under the Human Rights Act 1998 . The use of DNR notices will affect more than 50% of the UK 's population at some point in @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ procedure right . It 's vital that we give proper respect to people 's fundamental human rights at a time of great difficulty in life . TITLE: Brazil Tourism sets highest foreign visitor spending record

CONTENT: New to TTG ? Brazil Tourism sets highest foreign visitor spending record 04 Nov 2024 Tourism revenue in Brazil exceeds last year 's high , marking the highest figure in the country 's history . Brazil recorded an unprecedented US$ 5.409 billion in foreign exchange inflows from international tourists between January and September 2024 , marking the highest figure in the country 's historical data , which has been tracked since 1995 . According to new data released by the Central Bank , this total is 8% higher than the same period in 2023 and 1.8% higher than the corresponding period in 2014 , the year Brazil hosted the FIFA World Cup . This significant increase in tourism revenue also represents 78.2% of the total foreign exchange brought into the country in 2023 , which reached US$ 6.9 billion , setting a previous record . Notably , the US$ 5.4 billion brought in during the first three quarters of 2024 is 20% higher than the same period in 2019 , the last pre-pandemic year , reflecting the growing contribution of international tourism to Brazil @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , celebrated this milestone , emphasising the wide-reaching impact of foreign tourist spending on local economies : " The tourism industry is incredibly democratic . The money brought in by international visitors goes directly into our local communities , benefiting everyone from waiters and taxi drivers to street vendors on the beach . It 's an economic boost that supports jobs and income at the grassroots level . " Freixo also credited the success to the joint efforts of Embratur and Brazil 's tourism sector , noting that the country has consistently broken records in 2024 . " September saw the highest number of international tourist arrivals ever recorded , 26% higher than in September 2023 . The cumulative arrivals from January to September were also 12% higher than the previous year " , Freixo highlighted . He attributed this growth to strategic international promotion : " Embratur and the tourism trade have been working together , using data-driven strategies to promote Brazil abroad . These results are the outcome of a collaborative effort that promises to yield even greater success . When tourism thrives , everyone benefits . "

TITLE: Hopes for tourism boost as Loch Ness takes centre stage in new ...

CONTENT: A number of Highland locations were used in the filming of the show including Loch Ness , nearby Fort Augustus and parts of Lochaber . It is hoped the six-part drama will have a similar effect on tourism as shows such as Shetland and Outlander and bring more visitors to the area . Jenni Steele , Film and Creative Industries Manager at VisitScotland , said : " Loch Ness is famous throughout the world , attracting over 200,000 visitors a year from around the globe . " The eternal mystery surrounding the Loch makes it the perfect setting for a crime drama and Loch Ness , as well as the other locations featured , will further heighten its appeal among both other filmmakers and prospective holidaymakers . " We know that many visitors are inspired to explore Scotland after watching it on film or television and it 's likely this intriguing new TV series will have a similar effect . " Freda Newton , managing director of Loch Ness by Jacobite said she was looking forward to seeing the impact the new drama could have on tourism in the Highlands , particularly as @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ . " We 're hopeful that it will inspire people across the UK to consider visiting and exploring more of what 's on their doorstep as it is ever more beautiful in real life , " she added . " The ' Outlander effect ' has definitely provided the Highlands with a tourism boost as fans are eager to experience the spectacular scenery for themselves . We 've noticed an increase in interest from our international visitors inspired by the likes of Outlander and Disney 's Brave . "

TITLE: Hundreds of US gymnasts allege sexual abuse

CONTENT: Those that did ... to USA Gymnastics felt that their claims were ignored , as the governing body failed to alert police to the alleged crimes . " Some coaches are fired at gym after gym without being tracked or flagged by USA Gymnastics , or losing their membership with the organisation , " authors Tim Evans , Mark Alesia and Marisa Kwiatkowski report . Dozens of athletes say the powerful body - which determines who represents Team USA at the Olympic Games - never even replied to some of those making the claims . Gymnastics officials dispute that claims were kept quiet in order to preserve the reputation of the sport . " Nothing is more important to USA Gymnastics , the Board of Directors and chief executive Steve Penny than protecting athletes , which requires sustained vigilance by everyone - coaches , athletes , parents , administrators and officials , " the organisation said in a statement . " We are saddened when any athlete has been harmed in the course of his or her gymnastics career , " USA Gymnastics said , adding that mandatory background checks are now being performed on all coaches . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Larry Nassar , a former longtime doctor for USA Gymnastics , by women gymnasts accusing him of sexual assault , which he denies . Rachel Denhollinder told the BBC she was sexually assaulted when she was 15 years old and went to see Dr Nassar while he was working at Michigan State University . Ms Denhollinder said Dr Nassar sexually assaulted her while treating her , with her mother in the room . " He would position his hands in a way that she could not see what he was doing , " she said . " It was very difficult to reconcile the person he was supposed to be and with what he was doing and so the only conclusion I could come to was that there must be something wrong with me . " Dr Nassar pleaded not guilty last month to charges of sexually assaulting a minor .

TITLE: Former housekeeper cleans up with a rising star award

CONTENT: Former housekeeper cleans up at the Scottish Thistle Awards Hannah Cochrane , who started out cleaning hotel rooms when she was just 16 , has climbed the ranks and is the winner of our new Rising Star award Charles Pring Saturday November 23 2024 , 12.01am , The Times Hannah Cochrane worked her way up the ladder in a number of hotels Charles Pring Saturday November 23 2024 , 12.01am , The Times The inaugural Rising Star award is sponsored by HIT Scotland . It goes to Hannah Cochrane , who became sales and business development manager for the Scottish Event Campus this year . She is still only 30 , but reading her CV you 'd think she 'd been in hospitality for decades . From housekeeping and bartending to wedding planning and regional management , Cochrane has done it all . That 's one of the benefits of starting young , as Cochrane did , cleaning bedrooms at Mar Hall Golf & Spa Resort in her native Bishopton from the age of 16 . An " eye-opening " experience @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Wow , this industry is crazy -- but in all the best ways . ' I loved it . I was officially bitten by the hospitality bug . " Cochrane made the move to Glasgow , working her way up the ladder in a number of hotels . An events role at House for an Art Lover led to Cochrane becoming its wedding co-ordinator , planning hundreds of ceremonies in her three years in the job . Mar Hall resort , where Cochrane worked in her first hospitality job A two-year spell as regional business development manager at the restaurant chain Chaophraya was followed by a similar role at Dakota Hotels , where Cochrane developed an interest in sustainability practices , leading the group 's " green team " to a prestigious Green Tourism gold award . Two years as Glasgow ambassador at the Society for Incentive Travel Excellence then led to her present position : promoting Glasgow and the SEC to potential clients on the world stage . For someone as proud of her home town as Cochrane is , it 's a straightforward pitch @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ says . Advertisement Scottish Event Campus , where Cochrane is the business development manager at the age of just 30 MARC TURNER While the trajectory of Cochrane 's rise through the industry has been impressive , it is her efforts to give back to the sector that make her such a worthy winner . As hotels and restaurants stuttered following the pandemic , Cochrane launched Pure Buzzin ' , a podcast spotlighting the good work going on in Glasgow 's hospitality scene . She also started a networking event , Now That 's What I Call Networking , which holds quarterly meet-ups and has raised ? 20,000 and counting for Beatson Cancer Charity . Grateful for the opportunities that the industry has afforded her , Cochrane now dedicates herself to mentoring newcomers and promoting the sector , speaking in primary schools and universities . As if that is n't enough , she also has a residency at Bluedog in Glasgow : catch her singing Etta James and Otis Redding songs any Wednesday or Thursday .

URL: http://blogs.telegraph.co.uk/news/guywalters/100070237/the-media-should-be-ashamed-of-the-way-it-has-treated-chris-jefferies/
COUNTRY: gb

TITLE: The media should be ashamed of the way it has treated Chris Jefferies

CONTENT: Guy Walters is the author of nine books , which include four wartime thrillers and the critically acclaimed histories Hunting Evil and Berlin Games . Frustrated at the enormous amount of junk history around , Guy sees it as his personal mission to wage war on ignorance and misconceptions about the past . Guy is currently working on a new history of the Great Escape , and is also studying for his PhD at Newcastle University . His website is www.guywalters.com and is @guywalters on Twitter . The media should be ashamed of the way it has treated Chris Jefferies The news that retired schoolmaster Chris Jefferies has been released without charge on police bail is to be welcomed . Unfortunately , thanks to a disgraceful campaign of innuendo waged by the media , his name will forever be linked in the public 's mind with the murder of Joanna Yeates . Variously labelled as ' strange ' and a ' peeping tom ' , Mr Jefferies will find it hard , if not impossible , to return to his quiet and decent life . I 've already blogged at how Mr Jefferies ' apparent eccentricities have enabled him to @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ that the Attorney General , Dominic Grieve , has expressed his concerns over the way the media has reported the arrest . Mr Grieve should consider bringing actions against some media organisations for breaking the Contempt of Court Act , which is designed to ensure that an individual going through investigative and legal processes receives a fair trial . If Mr Jefferies is up for it , I expect he could bring his own legal actions as well . Perhaps the most appropriate opinion to quote is that expressed on Saturday by Greg Reardon , the boyfriend of Miss Yeates . " Jo 's life was cut short tragically but the finger pointing and character assassination by social and news media of as-yet innocent men has been shameful . It has made me lose a lot of faith in the morality of the British Press and those that spend their time fixed to the Internet in this modern age . I hope in the future they will show a more sensitive and impartial view to those involved in such heart-breaking events and especially in the lead up to potentially @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ . The media must learn to control themselves when presented with a figure such as Mr Jefferies . We do not need another victim in this already tragic case .

TITLE: 'Deeply shocking': Substitute teachers wrongly overtaxed after new ...

CONTENT: This site uses cookies to improve your experience and to provide services and advertising . By continuing to browse , you agree to the use of cookies described in our Cookies Policy . You may change your settings at any time but this may impact on the functionality of the site . To learn more see our Cookies Policy . A NUMBER OF substitute teachers have been paid less than their correct wage this month as a result of a taxation issue . The issue has affected a number of substitute teachers who were paid in the first and second pay runs of 2019 . The problem arose in the first pay runs of the New Year when the new PAYE modernisation system was applied for the first time . The payroll files that transferred to Revenue inadvertently included an end date for substitute staff which informed Revenue that they would not be paid under this employer number in the future . This notification caused Revenue to immediately reduce the tax credits and cut-off points to zero for this cohort of staff . It meant that when substitute teachers were next paid on @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ be applied to the salary . Any substitute teacher who enters the payroll for the first time after these two pay runs will not be affected , the Department of Education has said . " The payroll division and IT unit of the Department and Revenue are working in close cooperation to secure a permanent solution , " a spokesperson for the Department said . The spokesperson added that the issue is " being given the highest priority " . They said any substitute teacher affected by the issue can contact the Department seeking a letter to confirm that the issue arose as a result of PAYE changes being implemented by the Department and Revenue . The letter will also state that refunds of tax that have been deducted in error will be issued as soon as possible . ' Deeply shocking ' Fianna F ? il spokesperson on education Thomas Byrne has called on Minister for Education Joe McHugh to immediately provide pay cheques to substitute teachers whose pay has been impacted . " It is deeply shocking that the Minister @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ are expected to take unexpected deductions to their pay because of errors in the Department of Education 's own payroll software , " Byrne said . It is highly inappropriate that they are left waiting for refunds until the Department of Education and Revenue can get their act together . " At this stage , the Department of Education should simply write cheques to the teachers affected . URL: http://metro.co.uk/2017/03/05/the-enduring-thrills-of-sonic-all-stars-racing-transformed-readers-feature-6487152/
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: The enduring thrills of Sonic & All-Stars Racing Transformed ...

CONTENT: A reader is excited to find that Sega 's beloved kart racing is now working on Xbox One , thanks to backwards compatibility . I did n't have high hopes for the Xbox One 's backwards compatibility programme when it was first announced , but it has gradually proved my initial cynicism wrong . It 's a joy to be able to play games as popular as the original Mass Effect trilogy , and as niche as Lost Odyssey , without having to drag the trusty Xbox 360 out of the cellar . Last October , another game was added to the ' backcompat ' line-up that I was truly excited to play again . If it is universally accepted that Forza Horizon 3 is the best racing game on the platform , and quite possibly of the entire generation , then a strong case can be made that the console 's second-best racer is 2012 's kart-racing gem Sonic & All-Stars Racing Transformed . Developed by Sumo Digital , a third party UK dev comprised of racing game veterans , it was truly one of the great surprises of the last console generation . @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ that anyone would dismiss it at the time as being ' just another mascot kart racer ' . It 's been a long time since the days of Crash Tag Racing and Diddy Kong Racing , you crusty gaming curmedgeons . And Mario Kart is basically the only game in the genre still being made today . Well-reviewed and decently-purchased , it was the capper on a new golden era for Sega 's blue blur mascot . Sonic Generations and the Wii 's Sonic Colours had brought the spiky one back into our good graces , and this game , itself a refinement of 2010 's solid but inferior Sega & Sonic All-Stars Racing , was one that absolutely hooked me for months after its release . It looked like Steam was going to be the only way one could play the game in the current generation , but Microsoft wisely chose to make it available once again . The first thing you notice , and this is fitting considering who is in the title and on the box , is the speed . Sonic & All-Stars Racing @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ most thrilling racing games this side of WipEout or F-Zero . It makes Mario Kart feel like quicksand in comparison . Everything about the game is built on attaining speed . The key is the essential drift mechanic , which the gameplay is built on . When you drift , you build up boost , and the longer you can hold it before releasing , the faster the boost will be . Character stats determine the effectiveness of the drift boost . Boosts are also earned other ways . In classic Sonic prefab fashion , ? la pinball bumpers , speed bands , and trampolines that appear out of nowhere , the game places pink boost mats all over the tracks that emit a glow so you know they 're coming up . Every jump and transform is an opportunity to earn boost as well , by performing one or more tricks with a flick of the right stick . Finally , you can earn a risk boost in plane mode by flipping out of the way of an obstruction at the last split-second . Basically , you @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ which gives the gameplay an immediate and risky feel . You ca n't just drive normally and do well . You 're forced to focus on the boost mechanic . However , if you think you can just choose the characters with the highest boost stat and do well , think again . In true Sega arcade fashion , you have to memorise the diverse set of tracks first , and you 'll need a character with some handling in order to do that , because some of them are absolute doozies . Each track pays homage to the world of a game in Sega 's vast library of titles . There are , of course , Sonic tracks , but also tracks based on After Burner , Golden Axe , NiGHTS , The House Of The Dead , and my personal favourite , Jet Set Radio . Thrills abound in these tracks , and sometimes unexpectedly . The Shinobi track is a thrilling sprint through a Japanese forest and temple , which one would expect . However , you would n't necessarily expect the track based on @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ twisting , challenging , made-to-drift looper complete with a day-glo section , or the track based on forgotten GameCube curio Billy Hatcher And The Giant Egg to be one of the most challenging tracks in the game , with one or two monster turns that will easily send you careening to the sides of the track . The variety of the tracks is not just in their theme , but also how they represent the second major mechanic of the game . Tracks can support air , water , and ground sections , and each character 's vehicle can transform to accommodate the terrain . Tracks can also change lap-to-lap , bringing a new level of execution and memorisation to the equation , though I want to be clear that the water and air sections are not nearly as good as the ground sections and serve more to break up the tracks and offer some variety . This is probably why some of the tracks designed to use all three modes , such as the ones based on Panzer Dragoon and Skies Of Arcadia , feel a little @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ and succinct , like the After Burner track and the one based on Saturn-era niche title Burning Rangers , fare better . There are also too many Sonic tracks , five in all out of 21 total . Being a game built on Sega fan service , the cast reflects a bit of variety as well , like the company 's first mascot Alex Kidd , Beat and Gum from Jet Set Radio , Ulala and Pudding from Space Channel 5 , MeeMee and AiAI from Super Monkey Ball , and AGES , a fanboy 's meta wet dream that turns into the Daytona USA car , a Dreamcast controller , and the After Burner fighter jet . Like the tracks , the only issue is that there are just way too many Sonic characters , a whopping seven total out of a nearly 30 character roster . The Steam release benefitted from further updates and additional characters that were never made available on consoles , including Ryo from Shenmue , who drives a freaking arcade cabinet and was already playable in the first game . There @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ Rage , Virtua Fighter ( Akira and Jacky were playable in the first game ) , Phantasy Star , ToeJam and Earl , Shining Force , Vectorman , and Ecco the Dolphin . The Saturn/Dreamcast and original Xbox eras seem to be favoured here at the expense of the Mega Drive era , for better or worse depending on when you grew up . Oh , Danica Patrick and Wreck-It-Ralph are also in the game and Danica 's voice is n't even her . Marketing-driven decisions , these inclusions seem even more out of place now than they did in 2012 . Besides drifting and transforming , the third pillar of the gameplay is one that all great racing games hinge upon : skill . Part memorisation , part reaction time , and part studying the game 's mechanics and feel , Sonic & All-Stars Racing Transformed requires you to actually be good at the game . There are enough easy modes and other things to do if you 're just visiting , but if you want to hunker down , unlock all the characters , and get @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ to take some commitment . The game puts up one heck of a good fight on the harder difficulty modes and locks most of its greatest treasures behind thick walls of stars , which you accumulate as you play . The difficulty curve is much steeper than Mario Kart , but the rewards are worth it . Once you get all those tracks under your fingers and tucked inside your brain and find your perfect set of characters , Sonic & All-Stars Racing Transformed has stunning sights to behold . There 's the thrill of the neon-drenched rollercoaster of a level based on Sonic Colours , where you sometimes ca n't see what 's coming up right away and can fall off the track at anytime . Taking that track at top speed , drifting and hitting those boost mats like a pro , narrowly navigating each corner like a tightrope , and leaving your competition in the dust is a singular thrill . A similar sense of adrenaline and accomplishment awaits in the Shinobi track , which , when ran through optimally , hitting every drift @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ enough to give you whiplash . Trying to best your previous best time in the OutRun track , especially the mirrored one , never gets old , and taking in the visuals of the Golden Axe and The House Of The Dead tracks while avoiding fire and zombies is what video games are all about . The games offers you challenge tracks that make you run through the stages in different ways , and besides some of the omissions in characters and tracks and the fact that the ground sections are way better than the water and air sections , this is where the game can falter a bit . The challenge tracks are an absolute mixed bag . Some have you shooting missiles at a tank , which never got fun no matter what track it was on , while others are air-only challenges that make you go through rings , which only highlights the boring flying even more and unfortunately conjures up images of Superman 64 . Others , however , are even better than the normal races themselves . Drift challenges have you maintain drift @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ clock and it is an absolute blast , especially on a track like the Samba De Amigo one , which was made for it . Boost challenges have you putting time back on the clock every time you drift boost or hit a boost mat , which brings out the heart-pumping best in the Jet Set Radio stages , which are also the best stages for the traffic challenges that see you weaving in and out the way of computer-controlled bots . Even with its flaws , Sonic & All-Stars Racing Transformed is a complete racing experience . There 's so much to do and the unlocks are spaced out perfectly , just enough to get you to one more track before bedtime . Instead of aping Mario Kart 's tried-and-true gameplay , it opts for its own unique flavour of racing , one built on pure speed and thrills . It strikes that perfect balance of being a game for racing fans , but also a game about the universal joy of solid mechanics and great gameplay , which will appeal to non-racing game players alike . Fans @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ will too once you play it . Just , please , a little less Sonic next time . By reader David The reader 's feature does not necessary represent the views of GameCentral or Metro .

URL: https://www.leinsterleader.ie/news/newbridge/534078/foal-ventilator-given-to-naas-hospital-to-help-during-covid-19-crisis.html

TITLE: Foal ventilator given to Naas Hospital to help during ...

CONTENT: Foal ventilator given to Naas Hospital to help during Covid-19 crisis Donation in a good cause Naas General Hospital has taken delivery of a ventilator donated by a specialist equine medical professional . Patients who contract coronavirus generally have breathing problems . There is a worldwide shortage of mechanical ventilators which are used to help patients deal with respiratory problems after a tube is placed into the mouth and windpipe and connected to the ventilator . It was donated by Kevin Corley , a Ballysax , Curragh-based veterinary surgeon , who specialises in equine internal medicine and the critical care of foals and adult horses . He has a number of specialist qualifications and is based at Veterinary Advances , which provides a host of services including information technology aimed at the veterinarian and equine industry . The ventilator is about 15 years old and in perfect working order . Dr Corley said he handed it over having spoken to mechanical engineering staff at Naas General Hospital . " They were very pleased to receive it , " said Dr Corley , who added it was collected by Naas @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ . The ventilator itself has an interesting history " It was given to me when I worked in the UK , " said Dr Corley , who has been working in Ireland since 2006 . He acquired the piece of equipment having saved a foal from death for an anaesthetist who was working in the Royal Veterinary College outside London . The prestigious Royal Veterinary College is the oldest and biggest veterinary school in the United Kingdom . " The ventilator had been in a hospital originally and it was designed for people before it was used on foals and now it 's gone back to a hospital setting . " " In this context foals are not that different than humans partly because of similar weights . " " Hopefully , though , it wo n't be needed , " added Dr Corley . URL: https://www.screendaily.com/reviews/beyond-utopia-sundance-review/5178359.article
COUNTRY: gb
CONTINENT: Europe

TITLE: `Beyond Utopia': Sundance Review

CONTENT: ' Beyond Utopia ' : Sundance Review The desperate stories of North Koreans trying to defect - and the South Korean pastor who helps them Source : Sundance Film Festival ' Beyond Utopia ' Dir . Madeleine Gavin . US. 2022. 116 mins . It 's interesting to note that while the Sundance Film Festival website talks about this film being set in ' one of the most oppressive places on Earth ' , it never once mentions North Korea by name . Beyond Utopia is a gripping , moving , sweatily tense documentary that prises open the hidden fiefdom of Kim Jong-un through the misfortunate citizens fighting for their lives trying to escape it . Anxiety must be running high in Park City and beyond given how Sony was hacked when it dared to release a comedy gently mocking the dictator - this is a castigation . Anxiety , though , is an appropriate response to this nerve-shredding piece of work . Pastor Kim is relying on a network , his ' underground railroad ' which is shaky and susceptible to collapse Beyond Utopia is as tense , with equally @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ , which premiered in the same Sundance slot . It is much less formally composed . Anything could happen as Madeleine Gavin follows people who should n't be on camera to places they should n't be - the jungles of Vietnam and Laos at night , for example , or the living room of a North Korean defector anxiously trying to bring her teenage son to Seoul . Gavin does n't just edit and direct but lives this experience as South Korean Pastor Kim devotes himself publicly to helping North Koreans escape . There 's a religious , missionary element to Beyond Utopia which some may find offputting - its penniless subjects have to pay off so-called brokers every inch of their perilous route , financed by the church - but the curious will flock to see this film . Publicity could be dangerously hot and heavy for buyers who step up . The world does n't see much of North Korea : it 's hermetically-sealed off . Its occupants are taught that the country is a paradise on earth ; the rest of the world hears of nuclear @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ large-scale nationalistic ceremonies . Visitors to Pyonyang are closely watched . Mobile phones do n't work there . Pastor Kim has smuggled out footage , however , which shows desolate deprivation amid medieval agricultural farming methods . Gavin layers on historical and geographical context , which is necessary : defecting North Koreans are sent back from China if caught , for example , and have to take a circuitous secret route across that country and down through Vietnam and Laos before arriving in Thailand . Her film explains why . It also casts a light on the problem with religion in the world 's only Communist , Confucian , hereditary dictatorship . It 's complicated by the fact that Kim and his father and grandfather have appropriated the story of the Bible as their own dynastic mythology . No surprises as to who is God or Jesus in the North Korean origin story , but they 'd rather their subjects did n't realise where the source material comes from . Pastor Kim , a very brave man long before he shows his face to the camera , has devoted @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ 10,000 by his counting , in the decade since his son died . He even got his wife through . The Yalu River is the easiest crossing point to the Changbai mountains ( Pastor Kim broke his neck there once ) , but that 's only the beginning - there 's a high reward on every defector 's head for anyone who turns them in as they traverse through China and they 're dependent on middle-men every step of the way . If they go back they 'll be beaten , tortured , set to a Gulag , and perhaps killed -- there 's some chilling footage of public executions - but if they escape , their families will be targeted and ruined . Gavin hones in on two cases . The first is Soyeon Lee , a defector now living in Seoul and separated from her child a decade ago . How this came to happen is shown through an elegantly-rendered rotoscope-like animation . Initially poised and composed as she makes plans to bring the 17 year-old boy across the border , she - and we - become @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ turns . Then we have the Roh family , related to a defector called Hyukchang , who have made a run for it with only the clothes on their backs - mum , dad , two children , and 80 year-old granny who believes that Americans want to kill her , making her realtionship with the non-Korean-speaking Gavin a little tricky . They call Pastor Lee from the Changbai Mountains , wet and crying : help us . But Pastor Kim is relying on a network , his ' underground railroad ' which is shaky and susceptible to collapse . Madeline Gavin , whose last film City of Joy was made in the Democratic Republic of Congo , has done an exceptional job in pulling the disparate pieces of this film together . Multiple brokers have snuck out mobile phone footage , and where she ca n't go , Pastor Kim takes the camera for her . They 've built an alliance , Gavin and Pastor Kim and a defector called Heonseo Lee , who has written a book about her experiences also provides context and commentary . But @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ @ all the others who show their faces to the screen , and it could be for her too . This is a film you have n't seen before from a place you 'll never visit , a first-class example of bravery and reportage melding into an filmed testemant . It 's not just that it 's nailbiting . The unease lingers long after viewing , though , for every person associated with it .
